---
title: Linux CLI
---

## Prerequisites

Just like with anything we have to walk before we can run.

## Learning the Linux CLI

A thorough treatment of Linux CLI fundamentals geared towards the
beginner/novice is outside of the scope of this book. This book assumes some
familiarity with Linux and basic Linux CLI proficiency. That's not to say that
you _couldn't_ still work through this book and pass the [RHCSA](../appendices/glossary.qmd#rhcsa) exam, but be
forwarned the learning curve is steep. But, I can thoroughly endorse any of the
following resources to help you get up to speed:

1. [Learning the Shell][1] from **LinuxCommand.org** and [The Linux Command
  Line][2] by William Shotts

::: {.callout-tip}
This is my #1 go-to recommendation for beginners that want to learn
Linux and the Linux CLI. This is where I first learned Linux and the Linux CLI
many years ago.
:::

2. [Command Line Crash Course][3] from **Learn Python the Hard Way** by Zed Shaw
3. MIT **Missing Semester** Series: [Introduction to the Shell][4] &
  [Command-line Environment][5]
  
[1]: https://www.linuxcommand.org/lc3_learning_the_shell.php
[2]: https://www.linuxcommand.org/tlcl.php
[3]: https://learnpythonthehardway.org/book/appendixa.html
[4]: https://missing.csail.mit.edu/2026/course-shell/
[5]: https://missing.csail.mit.edu/2026/command-line-environment/

Becoming proficient in the Linux CLI is a pre-requisite to learning RHEL and
preparing for the [RHCSA](../appendices/glossary.qmd#rhcsa) exam. If this is you, definitely check out the
resources above. It's probably only a few days or weeks of effort to get up to
speed on the basics. Then come back and work through the rest of this book.

### Other notable mentions

There are some other free Linux learning resources worth mentioning. Some of
these are other projects I'm involved with.

4. [Linux Upskill Challenge][6] I've contributed to this project as an editor
5. The [Linux Administration Course][7] written by Scott Champine (het_tanis)
  and maintained by the [ProLUG][8] community (I'm a _ProLUG Engineer_, come say
  "Hi" in the [Discord][9]!)
6. [Linux Commands for Beginners][10] YouTube Playlist by [LearnLinuxTV][11]

[6]: https://linuxupskillchallenge.org/
[7]: https://professionallinuxusersgroup.github.io/lac/syllabus.html 
[8]: https://prolug.org/ 
[9]: https://discord.com/invite/m6VPPD9usw
[10]: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLT98CRl2KxKHaKA9-4_I38sLzK134p4GJ&si=fhFuZX3oHMk0puAO
[11]: https://www.youtube.com/@LearnLinuxTV
